ABOUT.CONSULTATION_CTA_DESC: With 15+ years of experience, I'm here for your child's health, development, and education. Book an appointment today.
ABOUT.DESCRIPTION: With over 15 years of experience, I apply the most current treatment methods for your children's health. We provide services according to American Academy of Pediatrics standards.

DR_PAGE.EDUCATION_TEXT: My university student sister enrolled me in primary school, while my university student brother enrolled me in prep school. As the capable child my dear father described, I managed all my subsequent enrollments myself. I met many of my lifelong friends at Rize Anatolian High School, the greatest value my education gave me. Again, I loved deeply and was deeply loved.
DR_PAGE.EDUCATION_TEXT2: In 2007, I graduated from Istanbul Medical Faculty, created by my distinguished professors who said 'If you don't have an idea about the diagnosis by the time the patient enters the room and sits down, quit this job.'
DR_PAGE.FAMILY_TEXT: I worked in the pharmaceutical industry for a while. During this time, following our generations-old family tradition, I had already fallen in love. Despite my feminist grandmother's attempts to instill anti-marriage sentiments throughout my childhood, in 2008 I married my dear love Mahir, whom I had known since I was twelve.
DR_PAGE.HEALTHY_CHILD_TEXT: As a physician with extensive practice with sick children until then, the upcoming birth of my daughter in 2014 pushed me with great curiosity into 'Healthy Child Medicine'. To academically strengthen my insatiable field readings, I started the Social Pediatrics Doctoral Program at Marmara University in 2019. I passed my doctoral qualification in 2021 and continue my academic life with my thesis process. In 2023, I graduated from Istanbul University's Child Development undergraduate program.
DR_PAGE.INTRO_TEXT: A brief look at my journey in social pediatrics and child development.
DR_PAGE.INTRO_WARM: Hello! I'm Dr. Özlem Murzoğlu. With over 15 years of pediatric experience, I provide scientific and compassionate care for your children in Ataşehir, Istanbul.
DR_PAGE.NEW_BEGINNING_TEXT: As an admired professor of mine said, 'To be a good doctor, enough patients and the right books are sufficient.' In July 2022, I left my work-school Marmara University Pendik Training and Research Hospital, where I had the opportunity to verify this experience.
DR_PAGE.NEW_BEGINNING_TEXT2: It was time to practice pediatrics with evidence-based, scientific data, with the facilities, time, and proximity to do so.
DR_PAGE.NEW_BEGINNING_TEXT3: I am starting a new journey with the support of my husband, daughter, mother, extended family, and my dear friends. From now on, I will welcome my children in my own clinic and enjoy being amazed by them every day despite my years of experience with children.
DR_PAGE.PEDIATRICS_TEXT: Far from all professional reasoning, simply because I love children very much, I started my Pediatrics specialization at Marmara University in 2011. In this institution where patients were treated in the number/variety of a Training and Research Hospital with a university approach, I got very tired and learned a lot.
DR_PAGE.PEDIATRICS_TEXT2: After completing my specialization, I returned to Marmara University Pendik Training and Research Hospital, which I had left due to a brief mandatory service adventure, and started working as a pediatrician. During the total of 11 years I worked in the emergency room, children's clinics, and all wards, I experienced great professional satisfaction with more cases and challenges than could be found anywhere else.
DR_PAGE.ROOTS_TEXT: I grew up in a small, unique and authentic Black Sea city, living through the famous local jokes. I shared my passion for trees and soil with my best preschool friends - my mother and grandfather. As the youngest of four children, I was deeply loved and I loved deeply, testing my dear sister's patience for years.

DIS_CIKARMA_SURECI.TITLE: Teething Process
DIS_CIKARMA_SURECI.EXCERPT: How does the teething process unfold in your baby? What should you watch for during this natural stage?
DIS_CIKARMA_SURECI.CONTENT:
Teething Process

The teething process usually begins around 6 months of age and continues until about 2 years of age. The first teeth usually start to appear around 6 months and are mostly the lower front teeth. Most children will have all their primary teeth by the age of 3.

Teething Symptoms

  • Mild discomfort
  • Crying
  • excessive drooling
  • Desire to chew hard objects
  • Mild swelling and tenderness in the gums

Recommended Strategies

✅ Recommended:

  • Teether toys (silicone or latex)
  • Cold washcloths
  • Gentle gum massage
  • Providing comfort and distraction
  • Maintaining regular sleep routines

❌ Not Recommended:

  • teething biscuits
  • herbal products
  • amber necklace
  • Numbing gels containing local anesthetic
Important Note: "If your baby's temperature is above 38.3 degrees Celsius, it is not related to teething."

It is important to meet with your pediatrician for individual guidance during the teething process.

COCUKLAR_ARASI_ZORBALIK.TITLE: Bullying Among Children
COCUKLAR_ARASI_ZORBALIK.EXCERPT: What is bullying, how can you recognize it, and how can we protect our children?
COCUKLAR_ARASI_ZORBALIK.CONTENT:
Bullying Between Children

Bullying is defined as "one child repeatedly attacking another child" and occurs when there is an imbalance of power.

Types of Bullying

  1. Physically (hitting, pushing)
  2. verbal (threat, taunt)
  3. Social (exclusion, spreading rumors)

Characteristics of Bullying

  • Can take place at school, on playgrounds, or online
  • It usually targets children with the following characteristics:
    • physically different
    • Shy or low self-confidence
    • having few friends
    • Perceived as "weak"

Suggestions for Parents

  • Have open conversations with children about bullying
  • Teach children how to respond:
    • Look into the eyes of the bully
    • stay calm
    • go away
    • Report incidents to trusted adults
  • Support kids who are being bullied
  • Understand the impact of children's behavior when they bully

Potential Consequences

  • Increased risk of substance abuse
  • academic difficulties
  • mental health problems
  • Increased likelihood of depression

The importance of communication, support and creating a safe environment for children is emphasized.

EMZIK_VE_EMZIK_BIRAKMA.TITLE: Pacifiers and Weaning Off Pacifiers
EMZIK_VE_EMZIK_BIRAKMA.EXCERPT: Guidance on pacifier use and strategies for giving it up at the right time.
EMZIK_VE_EMZIK_BIRAKMA.CONTENT:
Pacifiers and Pacifier Dropping

Pacifiers satisfy a baby's natural instinct to suck and can help soothe them. It may reduce the risk of Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS) when used before 6 months.

Potential Adverse Effects

  • Increased risk of ear infection after 6 months
  • Potential delays in speech development
  • Possible problems with teeth alignment if used after age 2

Recommended Pacifier Use

  • It can be used safely during sleep for children up to 1 year old.
  • It is best to start quitting between 6-12 months.
  • Limit daytime use, especially when the child needs to develop language skills

Quitting Strategies

  1. Gradually restrict pacifier use
  2. Provide alternative comfort objects
  3. Avoid using pacifiers as an initial response to discomfort
  4. Praise the child for not using a pacifier
  5. Consider a "farewell" ceremony or ritual to make letting go special

Important Recommendations

  • Use orthodontic pacifiers if continued after age 2
  • Never punish or shame the child during the quitting process
  • Choose appropriate timing (avoid major life changes)

The article emphasizes a gentle and supportive approach when helping children transition away from pacifier use.

TUVALET_EGITIMI.TITLE: Toilet Training
TUVALET_EGITIMI.EXCERPT: The right timing for toilet training and effective strategies.
TUVALET_EGITIMI.CONTENT:
Toilet Training

Toilet training usually occurs between 18 months and 4 years of age. Every child develops differently, so avoid comparisons.

Important Principles

  • Avoid starting during major life changes
  • Prevent withholding behaviors
  • Create a calm, non-threatening environment
  • Make sure the child's feet are supported while sitting
  • Provide a fiber-rich diet and adequate water intake

Toilet Training Stages

18 Months:

  • Start using appropriate toilet words
  • Make diaper changing fun
  • Encourage the child to say the diaper is wet/dirty

21 Months:

  • Get a suitable potty
  • Practice sitting on the potty together
  • Develop preliminary skills such as following instructions

2.5-3 Years:

  • Use books and videos for education
  • Practice with dolls
  • Encourage sitting on the potty for about 1 minute
  • Use positive reinforcement
  • Expect accidents and handle them without judgment

The article emphasizes patience, an individualized approach, and creating a positive learning environment for toilet training.

BIR_ERGENLE_ILETISIM_KURMAK.TITLE: Communicating with a Teenager
BIR_ERGENLE_ILETISIM_KURMAK.EXCERPT: Ways to build effective communication with adolescents.
BIR_ERGENLE_ILETISIM_KURMAK.CONTENT:
Communicating with a Teenager

Adolescents develop independent thoughts, feelings, and values. They are no longer children, but they are not yet adults either. Parents are concerned about their children's safety during this transition.

Main Recommendations

1. Be an Involved Parent

  • Show interest in the child's activities and friends
  • Communicate openly, honestly and respectfully
  • Set clear boundaries and expectations
  • Know what's happening at school and after school
  • Teach how to prevent violence safely

2. Positive Communication

  • Practice active listening without judgment
  • Ask questions about interests
  • Try sentences like "I understand you, but I see you differently..."
  • Avoid phrases like "That's stupid" or "You're wrong"

3. Understand Adolescent Characteristics

  • They are about new ways of doing
  • They focus on the present moment
  • They want role models
  • They want to be seen as competent

4. Security and Boundaries

  • Know where they are after school
  • Teach conflict resolution without fighting
  • Discuss expectations about:
    • Homework and school success
    • Outside hours
    • after school activities
    • don't spend money
    • vehicle security

The article emphasizes that understanding, respect and open communication are key to navigating the teenage years.

ARABA_GUVENLIK_KOLTUKLARI.TITLE: Car Safety Seats
ARABA_GUVENLIK_KOLTUKLARI.EXCERPT: Choosing and using the right car seat for child safety.
ARABA_GUVENLIK_KOLTUKLARI.CONTENT:
Car Safety Seats

Children under 13 must always travel in the back seat. Babies and toddlers should use rear-facing car seats until at least age 2, preferably until age 4.

Important Security Tips

  • Never place the car seat on the front seat with an active airbag.
  • Use the correct car seat for the child's weight, height and age
  • Make sure the seat belts fit properly around the child's body
  • Check vehicle seat installation carefully, use ISOFIX system or seat belts
  • Use a booster seat until the child is tall enough (approximately 145 cm) for a regular seat belt.

Important Safety Tips

  1. Avoid using second-hand car seats with unknown history
  2. Check expiration dates (usually 6 years after production)
  3. Make sure seat belts are positioned correctly:
    • The upper belt should go over the chest and shoulders
    • The lower belt should rest on the hips, not the abdomen
  4. The child should be able to sit with his back against the seat and his knees bent on the edge of the seat.

The article emphasizes that the use of appropriate car seats is critical for child safety during transportation by providing detailed guidance on selecting and installing appropriate car seats for different ages and sizes.

GUVENLI_UYKU.TITLE: Safe Sleep
GUVENLI_UYKU.EXCERPT: Safe sleep practices for babies and protection against sudden infant death syndrome.
GUVENLI_UYKU.CONTENT:
Safe Sleep and Protection from Sudden Infant Death

Main Recommendations

1. Always put babies to sleep on their backs

"Just implementing this measure reduces the likelihood of SIDS by half."

2. Never Sleep Together

  • Babies should sleep in their own bed, separate from parents
  • Sleeping in the same room (but in separate beds) is recommended, especially in the first 6 months

3. Create a Safe Sleep Environment

  • There should be no pillows, blankets or soft items in the crib.
  • Keep room temperature between 20-24°C
  • Use a firm mattress with tight-fitting sheets
  • Do not place the crib near heaters or windows

4. Additional Protective Measures

  • Breastfeed for at least the first 6 months
  • Use a pacifier when you put the baby to sleep
  • Make sure vaccinations are given on time
  • Avoid smoking and drinking alcohol during and after pregnancy

The article highlights that Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS) affects 1 in 5,000 babies and these guidelines can significantly reduce the risk.

SAGLIKLI_DISLER.TITLE: Healthy Teeth
SAGLIKLI_DISLER.EXCERPT: Strategies for protecting children's dental health and preventing cavities.
SAGLIKLI_DISLER.CONTENT:
Healthy Teeth

Tooth decay is the most common chronic childhood disease. 60% of children aged 12-18 have at least one cavity. Cavities occur when acid-producing bacteria erode tooth enamel.

Dental Health Recommendations

1. Fluoride Toothpaste

  • Start using fluoride toothpaste when the first teeth appear
  • Rice grain sized amount for babies
  • Pea-sized amount for children 3 years old

2. Systemic Fluoride Support

  • Recommended for babies 6 months and older
  • Fluoride supplementation of 0.25mg daily at 6 months, increasing to 0.50mg at 3 years of age
  • Challenging due to lack of fluoride specific products in Türkiye

3. Fluoride Varnish

  • Applied by dentists
  • First dental checkup is recommended at 1 year of age
  • Reapplied every 6 months

Important Notes

  • Excess fluoride can cause "fluorosis" (permanent tooth discoloration)
  • Early dental care is very important for lifelong dental health.
  • Cut down on sugary foods and maintain good oral hygiene

The article emphasizes preventive dental care that begins in infancy to protect against tooth decay.

EMZIK_PARMAK_EMME.TITLE: Pacifier and Thumb Sucking
EMZIK_PARMAK_EMME.EXCERPT: Guidance on pacifier use and thumb-sucking habits.
EMZIK_PARMAK_EMME.CONTENT:
Pacifier & Thumb Sucking

Sucking is a natural reflex for babies, it is even seen in prenatal ultrasound images. Sucking provides comfort and helps babies feel secure.

Pacifier Use Recommendations

  • Introduce it no earlier than 2 months
  • It may help reduce the risk of sudden infant death syndrome (SIDS) during the first year
  • It should be stopped after the age of 1 to prevent ear infections and dental problems.

Thumb Sucking Guide

  • It is natural behavior for children
  • Usually harmless until age 4
  • Most children quit between ages 2-3
  • May cause tooth and palate deformities after the age of 4

Suggestions for Parents

  • Do not scold children for thumb sucking
  • Praise children when they don't suck their thumb
  • Create a loving, safe environment
  • If behavior persists after age 4, seek professional help

Important Warnings

  • Never tie pacifiers around the baby's neck
  • Do not dip pacifiers in sugary substances
  • If behaviors persist beyond recommended ages, professional intervention is recommended

The article highlights that both pacifier use and thumb sucking are natural behaviors that provide comfort to children, but need to be monitored and potentially addressed if they persist beyond certain developmental stages.

KOLIK.TITLE: Colic
KOLIK.EXCERPT: Colic in babies and soothing strategies.
KOLIK.CONTENT:
Colic

Colic is a condition characterized by prolonged, intense crying in babies. It usually starts when babies are 2-3 weeks old and lasts until they are 3-4 months old.

Definition

Colic "describes crying spells that recur periodically and last for hours." It affects about 1 in 5 babies and is more common in first-born children and male babies.

Features

  • Babies usually cry 3-5 hours a day
  • Crying usually occurs at consistent times
  • Babies can produce significant amounts of gas
  • Crying may start and stop suddenly

Recommended Coping Strategies

  • Swaddle the baby (leaving the legs free)
  • Use a baby carrier
  • Rock the baby gently
  • Give a pacifier (after one month)
  • Massage the baby's back
  • Play rhythmic sounds
  • Change maternal diet if breastfeeding
  • Consider food changes
  • Take breaks and get support

The article emphasizes staying calm and understanding that these crying spells are a natural part of the baby's adjustment to the world outside the womb.

AYRILIK_KAYGISI.TITLE: Separation Anxiety
AYRILIK_KAYGISI.EXCERPT: Understanding separation anxiety in babies and ways to cope.
AYRILIK_KAYGISI.CONTENT:
Separation Anxiety

Separation anxiety usually begins around 6-8 months of age, peaks around 1 year of age, and begins to decrease around 15-18 months. Babies develop object permanence around 8 months and understand that things still exist when out of sight.

Features

  • Children develop intense attachment to one parent (usually the mother)
  • They cry when the parent is out of sight
  • They want to stay near the parent all the time

Suggestions for Parents

  1. Spend quality time with physical contact
  2. Play flirty games to teach that objects bounce back
  3. Create small breakup scenarios at home
  4. Make sure the child is comfortable before leaving
  5. Create a quick, consistent farewell ritual
  6. Avoid long, dramatic goodbyes
  7. Be consistent in delivering on your promise

Important Comment

"Separation anxiety is a natural developmental stage, and its presence is indicative of healthy child development."

The article emphasizes that this phase is normal and will resolve naturally as the child's memory and understanding improves.

PISIKLER.TITLE: Diaper Rash
PISIKLER.EXCERPT: How diaper rash develops in babies, its causes, and ways to prevent it.
PISIKLER.CONTENT:
Why Diaper Rash Happens and How to Prevent It

Diaper rash affects at least half of all babies at some point. Prevention strategies include:

Prevention Strategies

  1. Change diapers frequently (every 3-4 hours)
  2. Gently cleanse the skin
  3. Use barrier cream containing zinc oxide
  4. Choose highly absorbent diapers
  5. Let the skin breathe

Common Causes of Rash

  • Long-term exposure to urine and feces
  • Introducing solid food
  • Allergic reactions to diapers or wipes
  • Tight fitting diapers
  • Antibiotics
  • Bacterial or fungal infections

When to Consult a Doctor

  • If the diaper rash does not improve after 2-3 days
  • If the rash contains blisters, skin shedding, or open wounds
  • If the diaper rash is particularly painful
  • If accompanied by fever

The article emphasizes gentle care, keeping the diaper area clean and dry, and using protective barrier creams to prevent and treat diaper rash.

FAQ.A1: We provide services for all children aged 0-18 years at our clinic. We have specialized approaches for every age group from newborn period to the end of adolescence.
FAQ.A10: For the first examination, please bring your child's identification information, previous medical reports if any, vaccination card, list of medications used, and insurance documents if you have insurance.
FAQ.A11: Our standard examinations take an average of 30-45 minutes, while our Bright Futures comprehensive examinations take a minimum of 1 hour. First examinations usually take longer.
FAQ.A2: You can book an appointment online through our website, call us at 0216 688 44 83, or send a message via our WhatsApp line. Our online appointment system is available 24/7.
FAQ.A3: We do not have direct provision agreements with private health insurance companies. Our patients can benefit from reimbursement according to their policy terms using the private health insurance information form and freelance invoice we provide after the examination.
FAQ.A4: Bright Futures is a comprehensive health monitoring program developed by the American Academy of Pediatrics that tracks your child's physical, mental, and social development from birth to adolescence. It includes age-specific assessment forms and detailed examinations lasting at least 1 hour.
FAQ.A5: Our clinic operates by appointment system. For emergencies, we recommend visiting the nearest hospital emergency department. However, we provide emergency consultation services for our scheduled patients.
FAQ.A6: Yes, all routine vaccines according to the Ministry of Health's current vaccination schedule and special vaccines are administered at our clinic. Vaccination tracking is done digitally and reminder service is provided.
FAQ.A7: Yes, we provide online consultation services especially for our follow-up patients. We offer remote examination and consultation services via video conference.
FAQ.A8: Basic laboratory tests such as blood count, urinalysis, and rapid tests can be performed at our clinic. For more comprehensive tests, we refer to our partner laboratories.
FAQ.A9: You can cancel or change your appointment at least 24 hours in advance. You can make appointment changes through the online system, by phone, or by contacting us via WhatsApp.
FAQ.CONTACT_DESC: If you couldn't find the answer you're looking for, contact us.

HPV_VACCINE.content:
HPV Vaccine

Why Get Vaccinated?

HPV (human papillomavirus) vaccine can prevent infection with some types of human papillomavirus.

HPV infections can cause certain types of cancers including:

  • Cervical, vaginal, and vulvar cancers in women
  • Penile cancer in men
  • Anal cancer in men and women
  • Cancers of the tonsils, base of tongue, and back of throat (oropharyngeal cancer) in men and women

HPV infections can also cause anogenital warts.

HPV vaccine can prevent over 90% of cancers caused by HPV.

HPV is spread through intimate skin-to-skin contact or sexual activity. HPV infections are so common that nearly all people get at least one type of HPV at some point in their lives.

Most HPV infections go away by themselves within 2 years. But sometimes HPV infections last longer and can cause cancers later in life.

HPV Vaccines

HPV vaccine is routinely recommended for adolescents at 11 or 12 years of age to ensure they are protected before they are exposed to the virus. HPV vaccine may be given beginning at age 9 years, and is recommended for everyone through 26 years of age.

HPV vaccine may be given to adults 27 through 45 years of age, based on discussion between the patient and healthcare provider.

Most children who get the first dose before 15 years of age need 2 doses of HPV vaccine. People who get the first dose at 15 years of age or older, and younger people with certain immunocompromising conditions need 3 doses. Your healthcare provider can give you more information.

HPV vaccine may be given at the same time as other vaccines.

Talk with Your Healthcare Provider

Tell your vaccine provider if the person getting the vaccine:

  • Has had an allergic reaction after a previous dose of HPV vaccine, or has any severe, life-threatening allergies
  • Is pregnant. HPV vaccine is not recommended until after pregnancy

In some cases, your healthcare provider may decide to postpone HPV vaccination to a future visit.

People with minor illnesses, such as a cold, may be vaccinated. People who are moderately or severely ill should usually wait until they recover before getting HPV vaccine.

Your healthcare provider can give you more information.

Risks of a Vaccine Reaction

  • Pain, redness, or swelling at the injection site can happen after HPV vaccination.
  • Fever or headache can happen after HPV vaccination.

People sometimes faint after medical procedures, including vaccination. Tell your provider if you feel dizzy or have vision changes or ringing in the ears.

As with any medicine, there is a very remote chance of a vaccine causing a severe allergic reaction, other serious injury, or death.

What If There Is a Serious Problem?

An allergic reaction could occur after the vaccinated person leaves the clinic. If you see signs of a severe allergic reaction (hives, swelling of the face and throat, difficulty breathing, a fast heartbeat, dizziness, or weakness), call 911 and get the person to the nearest hospital.

For other signs that concern you, call your healthcare provider.

HPV_VACCINE.content:
# HPV Vaccine Information

## Why Get Vaccinated?

HPV vaccine can prevent infection with disease-causing types of human papillomavirus (HPV).

## Benefits
- Prevents over 90% of HPV-caused cancers
- Protects against genital warts
- Safe and effective

## Who Should Get It?
- Routinely recommended for 11-12 year olds
- Can be given from age 9
- Recommended for everyone through age 26

## Schedule
- 2 doses if first dose before age 15
- 3 doses if first dose at age 15 or older

## Possible Side Effects
- Pain, redness, or swelling at injection site
- Fever
- Headache

These are usually mild and go away on their own.

IHSAN_DOGRAMACI.NAME: Ihsan Dogramaci

CONTENT.TEXT_A: In the mid-twentieth century, while Türkiye was trying to heal its wounds as a young republic, its most valuable treasure, its children, was struggling with a silent epidemic. Infant mortality rates were alarmingly high, child health expertise (pediatrics) was almost non-existent, and modern, research-based medical education was a dream.
CONTENT.TEXT_B: A Doctor's Dream: The Sun Rising in the Middle of the Slums
CONTENT.TEXT_C: The first step of this dream was taken in the late 1950s, in a modest two-storey building in the middle of a slum in Ankara: Hacettepe Child Health Institute. This was the place where an understanding that had never been seen in Türkiye until that day sprouted. With the model he called "Integrated Medical System", Doğramacı believed that the hospital should not only be a place for treatment, but also a school where doctors and nurses are trained, and a laboratory where scientific research is carried out.
CONTENT.TEXT_D: While the children were being treated, their mothers were being given health and hygiene education, and future doctors were being equipped with the latest scientific data. This small institute quickly turned into a torch, and Hacettepe University, one of the most modern and prestigious universities in Türkiye, rose around it. Hacettepe, founded with the motto "Further, to the best", was a monument to Doğramacı's will to make the impossible possible.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: Visionary Who Brings Science to Anatolia
CONTENT.TEXT_F: With the great breakthrough initiated under his leadership, new universities were established all over Türkiye, especially in Anatolian cities where access to universities had been limited until then. This was not just to build a building, but also to open a door to the dreams of Anatolian youth. It enabled the introduction of certain standards for academic staff, the promotion of research and the strengthening of the role of universities in the development of the country.
CONTENT.TEXT_G: Thanks to this great transformation, the light of science had the opportunity to leave a few big cities and reach even the most remote corners of the country, from Erzurum to Van, from Sivas to Trabzon.
CONTENT.TEXT_I: In the last days of his life, his energy and dreams were still not exhausted. After the numerous state institutions he brought to Türkiye, this time he realized his dream of creating a center of excellence with the power of civil society. By donating his personal wealth and his family's wealth, he founded Bilkent University, Türkiye's first foundation university.
CONTENT.TEXT_J: Bilkent was the pinnacle of his educational philosophy. A world-class educational institution with financial and administrative autonomy, completely focused on research and merit... This has opened the doors of a new model, a new horizon, for higher education in Türkiye. He didn't just establish a university; At the same time, he was the pioneer of a tradition that did not rely on the state, created its own resources, and was based on scientific freedom.
CONTENT.TEXT_L: His legacy continues to live in the smile of a child healing in Hacettepe, in the dreams of a young man studying in Bilkent's library, and in the light shining in the laboratory of a university in Anatolia. He is an immortal proof of how one man can change the destiny of a country with dedication and a right vision.

IHSAN_DOGRAMACI.TITLE: Pioneer of Modern Pediatrics and University Reform

BODY.P1: The university system, on the other hand, was in a self-contained structure, far from embracing the potential of Anatolia, and was limited to a few large cities. A physician with a unique organizational genius and unshakable will, who would devote his life to changing this situation and building the foundations of health and science from scratch, would emerge: İhsan Doğramacı.
BODY.P2: When İhsan Doğramacı returned to Türkiye after the brilliant education he received abroad, he chose the most difficult one instead of choosing a comfortable career. His vision was not just to cure sick children, but to build a system that would give them a healthy future from the very beginning. He knew that the problem was not just a lack of medicine or doctors; The main problem was the absence of modern institutions that brought together science, education and service.
BODY.P3: His success at Hacettepe was just proof of how great İhsan Doğramacı's vision was. He believed that excellence should not be limited to a single campus, but should spread throughout Türkiye. In the 1980s, he undertook a huge mission to restructure Türkiye's dispersed and unstandardized higher education system as a whole.
BODY.P4: İhsan Doğramacı was a man who constantly "built" throughout his life. By stacking bricks on top of each other, he built not only buildings but also institutions that shaped generations and shaped Türkiye's future. He shaped global health by representing his country on international platforms such as the World Health Organization and UNICEF.

JONAS_SALK.NAME: Jonas Salk
JONAS_SALK.QUOTE: Well, I would say it belongs to the people. There is no patent. Could you patent the sun?
JONAS_SALK.TITLE: Developer of the Polio Vaccine

CONTENT.TEXT_A: In the mid-twentieth century, the world was in the grip of a terrible disease that returned like a ghost every summer and targeted especially children: Poliomyelitis. Parents were terrified that if their child developed a fever one morning, the next day they would end up in iron lungs or spend the rest of their lives in a wheelchair.
CONTENT.TEXT_B: Parks and swimming pools were empty, and social life was coming to a halt due to fear of the epidemic. In the midst of this global nightmare, a humble scientist, born to an immigrant family in New York, lit a ray of hope that would change the course of human history: Dr. Jonas Salk.
CONTENT.TEXT_C: First Steps to a Devoted Life
CONTENT.TEXT_D: When Jonas Salk entered medical school, he had one goal in mind: to use science as a tool to relieve human suffering. His passion for research prevented him from becoming a conventional doctor. Even in his early work developing a vaccine to prevent influenza, it was clear that he had a different vision. Instead of treating diseases one by one, he was looking for ways to eliminate them completely.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: When he established his own laboratory at the University of Pittsburgh in 1947, he set his sights on the greatest enemy of that time: polio. At that time, the general opinion in the scientific world was that an effective vaccine could only be made with a weakened "live" virus. However, Salk thought this approach could be risky. He took a more daring and previously untested route: using a "dead" virus to trick the body's immune system into creating a defense against the disease.
CONTENT.TEXT_G: Salk and his team locked themselves in the laboratory, almost isolating themselves from humanity. Working 16 hours a day, 7 days a week, they searched for the perfect formula to neutralize the polio virus with formaldehyde while ensuring that the virus did not lose its ability to mount an immune response.
CONTENT.TEXT_H: This was like looking for a needle in a haystack. Each failed attempt meant thousands more children suffered. But Salk's resolve was unshakable. He never lost faith, because the picture he envisioned was much stronger than the difficulties in the laboratory: a world where children ran and played freely in parks again.
CONTENT.TEXT_I: Finally, after thousands of experiments, they found that magic formula. The vaccine was first tested on animals and then on volunteers. The first human subjects included Salk, his wife, and three sons. By risking his own life and the lives of his family, he showed the world his unwavering belief in his invention.
CONTENT.TEXT_J: The Largest Medical Experiment in History and "The Sun is Rising"
CONTENT.TEXT_K: In 1954, the largest public health experiment in medical history began. Called "Polio Pioneers," nearly two million children volunteered to receive Salk's vaccine or a placebo. An entire nation was waiting with bated breath for the results.
CONTENT.TEXT_L: The wait ended on April 12, 1955. That day, the results of the vaccine trials were announced and summarized in one sentence: The vaccine was "safe, effective and potent." This news spread around the world like a cry of triumph. Bells rang in churches, people hugged each other and cried in the streets, headlines read "Salk's Vaccine Works!" he exclaimed. That day was one of the brightest victories of humanity against darkness with the power of science. The empire of fear created by polio began to collapse that day.
CONTENT.TEXT_N: Jonas Salk suddenly became one of the most famous people in the world. He was a hero now. On a television show, the host asked him the million-dollar question: "Who owns the patent for this vaccine?" This invention could make him richer than he ever imagined. But Salk's response became an immortal monument to his character and purpose:
CONTENT.TEXT_O: "Well, I can say it belongs to humans. It has no patent. Can you patent the sun?"
CONTENT.TEXT_P: These three short sentences sealed his legacy forever. For him, this vaccine was not a source of personal wealth, but a gift to humanity. Just like the sun, it should belong to everyone and be offered for the benefit of everyone. This sacrifice surpassed even his scientific genius, making him one of the most respected figures in human history.
CONTENT.TEXT_R: Jonas Salk continued to work for humanity for the rest of his life. The Salk Institute he founded is still one of the world's most important research centers in the field of biological sciences today.
CONTENT.TEXT_S: His story is the most inspiring proof of how one person's vision, perseverance and sacrifice can save millions of lives. It reminds us that the greatest success lies not in personal gain but in service to humanity. Dr. Salk not only defeated polio; At the same time, it set a shining and unshakable example of what the purpose of science should be.

LOUIS_PASTEUR.NAME: Louis Pasteur
LOUIS_PASTEUR.TITLE: Pioneer of Microbiology and Vaccine Science

CONTENT.TEXT_A: In the nineteenth century, the world was a place where invisible, unnamed murderers ruled. The simplest wound could turn into a deadly infection, a mother in labor could die from a mysterious malady called "puerperal fever", a sip of milk could make a child sick, and the bite of a rabid dog was a sure sign of a death filled with terrible pain.
CONTENT.TEXT_B: The Path from Brush to Microscope
CONTENT.TEXT_C: His first major struggle was over a problem that threatened France's famous wine industry. The wines were going sour and spoiling for no reason, without anyone knowing why. Scientists at the time believed that this was a simple chemical reaction. Pasteur turned his microscope to wine barrels and saw the truth: The source of the problem was the beneficial yeasts that fermented the wine, as well as the undesirable microorganisms that soured it.
CONTENT.TEXT_D: His solution was revolutionary: heating the wine just enough to kill the harmful microbes in it without spoiling its taste. "Pasteurization", which is known by its name today, was born. With this simple but ingenious method, he would save not only wine, but also beer, milk and therefore countless human lives.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: "Life comes from Life": The Triumph of Swan Neck Bottles
CONTENT.TEXT_F: Pasteur's real revolution began when he challenged the theory of "spontaneous generation" that was deeply rooted in the scientific world at that time. According to this theory, living organisms could arise spontaneously from inanimate matter, just as maggots reproduce from rotting flesh. Pasteur believed this was impossible: “Omne vivum ex vivo” – “All life comes from life.”
CONTENT.TEXT_G: The experiment he designed to prove this claim is one of the most elegant and convincing experiments in the history of science. He prepared glass balloons in which he filled broth and bent their necks in an S-shape like the neck of a swan. Air could enter through this curved path, but the dust and germs in the air were trapped in the fold. Conclusion? The broth in the swan-necked bottles remained intact for months, without a single germ growing. But once the neck of one of the bottles was broken, the broth became cloudy and full of germs within hours.
CONTENT.TEXT_H: That day, Pasteur proved to the whole world that diseases and decay were not caused by the air or evil spirits, but by living things that are around us at all times, invisible to the naked eye, namely microbes. Germ theory was born and the door to modern medicine was wide open.
CONTENT.TEXT_I: The Weakened Enemy: The Birth of Vaccinology
CONTENT.TEXT_J: After this victory, Pasteur took his fight against invisible enemies directly to the front. He solved a disease that destroyed the silkworm industry and tackled diseases such as anthrax and chicken cholera that decimated animals. In the process, he made another genius invention of his career: Attenuation.
CONTENT.TEXT_K: While studying chicken cholera microbes, he realized that an old bacterial culture he had forgotten in the laboratory when he returned from vacation did not make the chickens sick, but rather protected them against the deadliest form of the disease. He realized that by weakening the enemy, he could teach the body to defeat it and recognize it the next time it encountered it. This was the basic principle of vaccinology. With this method, he would first develop a shield against anthrax and then against one of humanity's greatest fears.
CONTENT.TEXT_M: No disease was as terrifying as rabies. The bite was a death sentence that began weeks or months later and ended with a terrible ending. Although Pasteur could not isolate the rabies microbe, he managed to weaken the virus and develop a vaccine by drying the spinal cords of sick rabbits. However, this vaccine has never been tested on humans.
CONTENT.TEXT_N: In July 1885, fate confronted him with his most difficult decision. A nine-year-old boy, Joseph Meister, bitten 14 times by a rabid dog, was brought to him by his mother in despair. If he did not treat the child, it was certain that he would die. If the vaccine didn't work or made it worse, it could be labeled a killer.
CONTENT.TEXT_O: Pasteur made the biggest gamble of his life and with trembling hands injected the first dose into the child. After 13 days of injections, the whole world waited with bated breath. Joseph Meister lived. Pasteur saved not just a child, but hope.
CONTENT.TEXT_Q: His legacy reminds us that even in the darkest moments, the light of reason and science can guide us. Pasteur's life is a living proof of what curiosity, perseverance and the desire to serve humanity can achieve.

BODY.P1: Illnesses were seen as a punishment from God, a result of bad weather (miasma), or imbalances in the body. In the midst of this invisible empire of terror, a stubborn chemist born as the son of a tanner in a small town in France would launch a one-man war against humanity's greatest enemies and lay the foundations of modern medicine: Louis Pasteur.
BODY.P2: Louis Pasteur's first passion was art, not science. He was a talented painter who painted portraits of his family and friends in his youth. However, the fire of curiosity within him soon drew him to the mysterious world of crystals and the magic of chemistry. What distinguished him from other scientists was clear from the very beginning: He was not interested in theories that remained only in the laboratory. He wanted science to touch the lives of ordinary people, to save the farmer's crop, the winemaker his barrel, the doctor his patient.
BODY.P3: Louis Pasteur was much more than a scientist. He was a hero who brought the power of science to the service of humanity and changed the course of millions of lives with his determination and courage. Even today, the Pasteur Institute he founded is at the forefront of the fight against diseases, following the path he paved.

MALALA_YOUSAFZAI.NAME: Malala Yousafzai

CONTENT.TEXT_A: In the early twenty-first century, a dark shadow cast a dark shadow over Pakistan's idyllic Swat Valley. The Taliban regime traded the beauty of mountains and rivers for fear, music and television for bans, and most importantly, girls' pens and books for ignorance. Schools were being closed one by one, and the dreams of a generation were being blown up with dynamite.
CONTENT.TEXT_B: Diary of Gul Makai: The Light Shedding in the Darkness
CONTENT.TEXT_C: For Malala, school was not just a building with four walls; It was the epitome of freedom, dreams and the future. The daughter of her father, Ziauddin Yousafzai, an education activist and a school principal, she grew up believing that knowledge was the greatest power. When the Taliban locked the doors of their school, they thought they would also lock Malala's soul. But they were wrong.
CONTENT.TEXT_D: When the BBC Urdu service was looking for a blogger to describe life in the Swat Valley, it was eleven-year-old Malala who shouldered the task that no one else had the courage to undertake. Under the pseudonym "Gül Makai", she put into words how daily life turned into a nightmare, the fear she felt on the way to school, her longing for her favorite lesson, and how her desire to hold a pencil turned into a rebellion.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: The innocent but determined lines of that little girl crossed the walls of the valley and reached the world. Gul Makai's diary was a flashlight shining into the darkness of the Taliban. When her true identity was soon revealed, she became not only her father's daughter, but also the voice of all girls in Pakistan.
CONTENT.TEXT_F: The Scream They Want to Silence
CONTENT.TEXT_G: As Malala's voice grew louder, the owners of darkness became more disturbed. A child's courage was more dangerous than their weapons. They decided to silence him. On October 9, 2012, as she was returning home on the school bus, a masked assailant stopped the bus and asked: "Which one of you is Malala?" Without waiting for an answer, he pulled the trigger.
CONTENT.TEXT_H: That bullet targeted not only Malala, but everyone in the world with conscience and hope. Their goal was to destroy a symbol, to silence a scream forever. The world held its breath as Malala entered a battle between life and death with her head wound. The attackers thought his story would end there. However, this was just the beginning of the story.
CONTENT.TEXT_I: A Voice Stronger than Lead: A Global Awakening
CONTENT.TEXT_J: Instead of silencing Malala, the Taliban's bullet turned her into a megaphone that carried her voice a million times farther. When she miraculously clung to life after months of treatment in England, she was no longer a little girl from Swat Valley, but the voice of a global conscience.
CONTENT.TEXT_K: He made his first public speech after his return to life at the United Nations podium on his 16th birthday. The whole world was listening to him. But there was no revenge or hatred in his voice. On the contrary, there was love, forgiveness and reconciliation. The following words he said on that historic day became his manifesto:
CONTENT.TEXT_L: "Weakness, fear and despair died that day. Strength, courage and enthusiasm were born that day... Terrorists are afraid of my books and my pens. The power of education scares them... One child, one teacher, one book and one pen can change the world."
CONTENT.TEXT_M: Nobel Peace Prize and the Global Struggle
CONTENT.TEXT_N: In 2014, at the age of 17, he was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for his efforts in the fight of children and young people against oppression and for everyone's right to education. He made history as the youngest person to win this award.
CONTENT.TEXT_O: By establishing the Malala Fund, she began working for the education of girls around the world. She opened schools in Syrian refugee camps, campaigned for girls kidnapped by Boko Haram in Nigeria, and fought for the right to education everywhere from Brazil to Kenya.
CONTENT.TEXT_P: He graduated from Oxford University in 2020 with a degree in Philosophy, Politics and Economics. She continued her struggle for education in her own life and continued to be a role model for girls.
CONTENT.TEXT_R: Her story proved that courage is not measured by age, justice cannot be limited by gender, and a pen can indeed be more powerful than a sword. Wherever in the world a girl goes to school today, she becomes a part of Malala's struggle.

MALALA_YOUSAFZAI.TITLE: A Global Voice for Girls' Right to Education
MALALA_YOUSAFZAI.QUOTE: One child, one teacher, one book, and one pen can change the world.

BODY.P1: In this suffocating atmosphere, where even adults were afraid to speak in whispers, an eleven-year-old girl who loved her school and books more than anything else would shout to the whole world, "I am not afraid," and become the living legend of how a single voice can shake a tyranny: Malala Yousafzai.
BODY.P2: Malala Yousafzai reminded us how much light a single candle can create even in the darkest moments, that a child's voice can be more effective than even the most powerful weapons, and that education is the greatest victory against ignorance and hatred.

NILS_ROSEN.NAME: Nils Rosen
NILS_ROSEN.QUOTE: Every minute matters for a premature baby. Modern technology gives them that time.
NILS_ROSEN.TITLE: A Quiet Pioneer of Modern Neonatal Care

CONTENT.TEXT_A: Premature Baby Problem and Search for Solutions
CONTENT.TEXT_B: In the early 20th century, premature babies had a very low chance of survival. Temperature control provided by traditional methods was inadequate, the risk of infection was high, and babies often lost their lives. When Rosen began his residency in pediatrics in 1912, he had the opportunity to observe this drama up close.
CONTENT.TEXT_C: When he started working at Stockholm Children's Hospital in the 1920s, he was deeply affected by the high rate of premature infant mortality in the hospital. This experience defined an area of ​​research that would shape the rest of his life: developing technological solutions that would increase premature babies' chances of survival.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: The year 1928 was the turning point in Rosen's career. Realizing the inadequacy of the primitive heating systems used until then, Rosen designed a new system that provided precise temperature control, offered sterilization and allowed continuous observation of the baby.
CONTENT.TEXT_F: "The duty of medicine is to protect the most vulnerable. Premature babies come first. We must provide them with not only a warm but also a sterile environment, controlled humidity and constant care."
CONTENT.TEXT_G: The incubator system developed by Rosen was the prototype of the modern incubators used today. Features such as automatic temperature control, air filtration, visual control thanks to glass walls and ease of sterilization were revolutionary for that period.
CONTENT.TEXT_I: His work titled "Premature Baby Care", published in 1935, became a milestone in the field of neonatal medicine. In this work, he put forward pioneering views not only on incubator technology but also on feeding premature babies, protecting them from infections and monitoring their development.
CONTENT.TEXT_J: Rosen's research has shown that premature babies require comprehensive approaches not only to temperature control but also to nutritional support, oxygen therapy, and infection control. This holistic perspective laid the foundations of modern neonatal intensive care units.
CONTENT.TEXT_L: Rosen, who became professor of pediatrics at Karolinska Institute in 1940, was influential not only as a clinician but also as an educator. The students he trained spread the methods he developed to other countries in Europe as well as Sweden.
CONTENT.TEXT_M: During the Second World War, the programs he developed in the field of child health helped reduce infant mortality rates even under the adverse conditions of the war. Rosen's work was implemented not only in Sweden, but also in other countries that experienced difficulties with child care in wartime conditions.
CONTENT.TEXT_N: Heritage and Its Effects on Today
CONTENT.TEXT_O: Every premature baby born in the world today benefits, in some sense, from Rosen's vision. By combining the basic principles he identified with electronic control systems and advanced technology, modern incubators have increased the survival rate of premature babies to over 90%.

BODY.P1: Nils Rosén, the owner of one of the most important inventions in the history of medicine that enabled premature babies to survive, is a Swedish pediatrician who laid the foundations of modern neonatal medicine. The incubator systems he developed have saved the lives of millions of premature babies and have become an indispensable part of today's neonatal intensive care units.
BODY.P2: Born in Växjö, Sweden, in 1884, Nils Rosén drew attention with his interest in medicine and technology since his childhood. While studying medicine at Karolinska Institute, she focused especially on problems in the field of child health and sought solutions to the difficulties experienced by premature babies.
BODY.P3: By the time he passed away in 1963, the technologies developed by Nils Rosén had become widely used worldwide. The basic principles of his incubator designs continue to form the basis of today's neonatal technology.
BODY.P4: Nils Rosén not only developed a technology, but also changed the perspective on premature babies. He showed that these little babies were not just "born prematurely" but individuals who required special care, and sought ways to provide them with the medical support they deserve.

TURKAN_SAYLAN.NAME: Turkan Saylan

CONTENT.TEXT_A: Fighting Leprosy: The War Against Social Prejudices
CONTENT.TEXT_B: Saylan, who rebelled against the exclusion of leprosy patients from society and abandonment by their families, worked tirelessly to explain that the disease was treatable and its contagiousness was not as high as thought. He was shaking hands with his patients, embracing them, showing how lonely and needy these people, whom society feared, actually were.
CONTENT.TEXT_C: When he became the chief physician of Istanbul Leprosy Hospital in 1988, he made the hospital much more than a treatment center. This place turned into a center of hope where patients were reintegrated into society, vocational training courses were given, and human dignity was rebuilt.
CONTENT.TEXT_D: ÇYDD and Snowdrops: An Educational Revolution
CONTENT.TEXT_E: Saylan did not see girls' education as just a matter of rights; This was also a necessary step for Türkiye's development and modernization. He emphasized the importance of equality between men and women at every opportunity, saying, "A country's level of development is measured by the status of its women."
CONTENT.TEXT_F: "If you educate a girl, you will save a family, a society. Because an educated mother will also educate her children. This is a chain reaction and the key to social transformation."
CONTENT.TEXT_G: ÇYDD's activities were not limited to just giving scholarships. He was active in many areas such as dormitory construction, library establishment, teacher training and health screenings. Saylan was personally going to the most remote villages of Anatolia, talking to families, and trying to persuade girls to study.
CONTENT.TEXT_I: Saylan, who was also successful in his academic career, received the title of associate professor in 1976 and professor in 1982. He published many scientific articles and represented Türkiye in international congresses. However, the most important thing for him was to put science at the service of society.
CONTENT.TEXT_K: When she passed away on May 18, 2009, she left behind thousands of educated young girls, countless life stories and an unshakable legacy of struggle. Tens of thousands of people attended his funeral; His students, his patients, his colleagues and everyone who believed in his struggle were there.

TURKAN_SAYLAN.TITLE: Defender of Science, Education, and Republican Values
TURKAN_SAYLAN.QUOTE: My only concern is that our girls can receive an education.

BODY.P1: In Türkiye's modern history, there are very few people who managed to combine science, education and the struggle for human rights in a single life. Türkan Saylan was one of these rare people. He was not only a doctor, but also an architect of social transformation, a beacon of hope for thousands of girls and a tireless defender of the values ​​of the Republic.
BODY.P2: Born in Istanbul in 1935, Türkan Saylan swore that she would not remain silent in the face of the inequalities she witnessed as a child and especially the injustices experienced by girls. This oath was the beginning of a journey that would take him first to the doors of the medical school and then to the most remote corners of Türkiye.
BODY.P3: Türkan Saylan, who graduated from Istanbul University Faculty of Medicine in 1963, specialized in skin and venereal diseases. However, his real battle would begin with leprosy patients, whom society saw as "cursed". When he started working at Istanbul Leprosy Hospital in 1976, he saw that patients were struggling not only with physical but also social isolation.
BODY.P4: The Contemporary Life Support Association (ÇYDD), which she founded in 1989, was the greatest work of Türkan Saylan's life. The main purpose of the association was to ensure access to education, especially for girls, and to spread the secular, contemporary values ​​of the Republic. With the "Snowdrops" project, she provided scholarships to girls in the remotest corners of Anatolia, enabling them to continue their education.
BODY.P5: Türkan Saylan defended the superiority of scientific thought and reason throughout her life. He fought against superstitions and dogmas. He explained the importance of science and education at every opportunity, in conferences and television programs. He encouraged young people with the words "Don't be afraid, think and produce."
BODY.P6: Being detained within the scope of the Ergenekon case in 2009 and the trauma she experienced while receiving cancer treatment worsened Türkan Saylan's health. However, he did not give up his struggle until his last breath. Even from his hospital bed, he managed ÇYDD's work and asked about the situation of his scholarship students.
BODY.P7: Türkan Saylan's legacy still lives on today. ÇYDD continues its work, Snowdrops grow, stand on their own feet and continue to walk on the path opened by it. He was not just a doctor, but an education revolutionary, a human rights defender and an indomitable soldier of the Republic.

URSULA_LEGUIN.NAME: Ursula K. Le Guin
URSULA_LEGUIN.TITLE: The Writer Who Changed Worlds Through Imagination

CONTENT.TEXT_A: In the mid-twentieth century, the literary world was sharply divided. On one side was mainstream literature, which was considered "serious", and on the other was "genre" literature, which was often seen as escapist fantasy, full of rockets, monsters and magic swords.
CONTENT.TEXT_B: From the Cradle of Anthropology to the Peak of Literature
CONTENT.TEXT_C: Ursula did not grow up in an ordinary home. His father was the famous anthropologist Alfred Kroeber and his mother was the writer Theodora Kroeber. His childhood was spent with conversations about different cultures, myths, languages ​​and social structures. Their home was like a library filled with thousands of years of stories of humanity. This intellectual heritage formed his writing DNA. For Le Guin, trying to understand the "other" was not a curiosity, but a way of life.
CONTENT.TEXT_D: When he first stepped into the world of literature, his works were frequently rejected by publishing houses. Because his stories did not fit into the usual molds. It is not about spaceships and dragons, wars and heroism; She used it as a stage to question gender, anarchism, capitalism, religion, language and power. He was not running away from reality, he was inventing new worlds to understand reality.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: Earthsea: The Power of Magic and True Names
CONTENT.TEXT_F: He revolutionized fantasy literature with "A Wizard of Earthsea" published in 1968. In this genre, which had been dominated by white heroes until then, Le Guin introduced Ged, a dark-skinned hero, to the readers. More importantly, he transformed the magic system in Earthsea from a mere display of power to a deep philosophy. Casting spells in this world was possible by knowing the "true name" of beings. Giving a name to something meant having power over it, and with this power came tremendous responsibility.
CONTENT.TEXT_G: Le Guin whispered this to her readers: Language is not just a means of communication; It is a force that shapes the world and creates reality. The greatest wars are not against enemies outside, but are fought against one's inner shadow, arrogance and fears. Earthsea was not a simple adventure story, but a journey of balance, wisdom and self-knowledge.
CONTENT.TEXT_H: The Dispossessed and the Left Hand of Darkness: Worlds That Free Minds
CONTENT.TEXT_I: Le Guin continued to push the boundaries of minds in the science fiction arena. In his 1969 novel "The Left Hand of Darkness," he described a planet called "Winter" whose inhabitants were genderless for most of the year and only acquired male or female characteristics during certain periods. With this daring thought experiment, he radically shook gender roles, identity perception and prejudices, which are one of the most basic building blocks of society. By uttering the sentence "The King was pregnant" from the mouth of an ambassador, he broke down all the walls in the reader's mind.
CONTENT.TEXT_J: In his novel "The Dispossessed", he examined the contrast and interaction between an anarchist lunar society and a capitalist world. But he never offered simple answers. He did not hesitate to show both the stifling aspects of the anarchist community and the freedoms offered by the capitalist world. For him, utopia was not a paradise to be reached, but an "ambiguous" quest that was constantly questioned, full of contradictions.
CONTENT.TEXT_K: "Imagination is the Greatest Tool of Freedom"
CONTENT.TEXT_L: "Hard times lie ahead. We need artists, writers, people who believe that imagination is a larger reality... Resistance and change often begin in art, and often in our art, the art of words."
CONTENT.TEXT_M: For him, imagination was not a childish distraction but the most important, most revolutionary tool adults had. It was a way to show that there was an alternative to the reality imposed on us, to whisper that a more just, freer and more balanced world was possible.

BODY.P1: Science fiction and fantasy were seen as a playground for simple battles of good and evil, dominated by mostly male writers and readers. In the midst of these rigid and narrow patterns, a wise woman who believed in the power of words would not only transform these types, but also turn them into one of the most profound tools for understanding the human condition: Ursula K. Le Guin.
BODY.P2: Even at the peak of her career, Ursula K. Le Guin chose to stay outside the system. He was a thinker and an activist as well as a storyteller. In his speech accepting the National Book Award in 2014, he raised a bold voice against the commercial ambitions that have taken over the literary world and the stifling effect of capitalism on art:
BODY.P3: Ursula K. Le Guin's legacy is not limited to the dozens of books she wrote. His real legacy is the windows he opened in the minds of millions of readers. He instilled in us the courage to understand what is different, to embrace the unknown and to question our own worlds. While he was building new universes with words, he actually enabled us to see our own universe better.

VIRGINIA_APGAR.NAME: Dr. Virginia Apgar

CONTENT.TEXT_A: Until the mid-twentieth century, the birthing room was a battlefield focused solely on the mother's struggle for survival. When the baby was born, all attention and effort was focused on stopping the bleeding and stabilizing the mother. The defenseless being born at those moments was often put aside, left alone with an uncertain fate whether it took a breath or made a cry.
CONTENT.TEXT_B: “Blue babies” were common, many passing away quietly, and this was considered a “natural” and painful part of childbirth. There was no simple method to standardize what to look for and how to intervene in those critical first moments. In all the complex knowledge of medicine, the most fundamental moment of life was consigned to unsystematic confusion.
CONTENT.TEXT_C: From Surgery to Anesthesia: A Path Full of Obstacles
CONTENT.TEXT_D: In those years, anesthesia was an area that was performed by nurses and was not given much importance. Apgar fought to turn this field into a medical specialty. He focused specifically on obstetric anesthesia, that is, anesthesia during childbirth. While attending thousands of births, he observed firsthand how the medications the mother took affected the baby.
CONTENT.TEXT_F: Apgar was enlightened by a simple question asked by a medical student at breakfast one day: "What should we do to evaluate the condition of a newborn baby?" Until that moment, there was no clear, standard answer to this. Apgar began to scribble on a piece of paper whatever came to her mind at that moment. A baby needed five simple criteria that could quickly and objectively assess its health immediately after birth:
CONTENT.TEXT_P: Each criterion would be given 0, 1 or 2 points. This evaluation, out of 10 in total, would be repeated in the first and fifth minutes after birth. This much! It was simple, easy to remember, and incredibly effective. This catchy system, consisting of the letters of the surname, went down in history as the "Apgar Score".
CONTENT.TEXT_Q: This score was a revolution. Now every nurse and doctor in the delivery room knew what to do as soon as they looked at the baby. It was immediately clear that a low-scoring baby needed urgent intervention.
CONTENT.TEXT_R: From Science to Advocacy: Angel of Those Born Imperfect
CONTENT.TEXT_S: Apgar did not stop after ensuring that his scoring system became a standard around the world. Instead of retiring, he embarked on the second biggest challenge of his life. He decided to study birth defects and intellectual disabilities and became head of the National Birth Defects Foundation (March of Dimes).
CONTENT.TEXT_T: He traveled throughout the country and raised public awareness on issues such as the importance of the rubella vaccine and the dangers of drug and alcohol use during pregnancy. She fought to make not only the moment of birth, but also the process before birth safer.

VIRGINIA_APGAR.TITLE: The Pioneer Who Gave Newborn Medicine a Language for the First Minutes
VIRGINIA_APGAR.QUOTE: Every baby deserves the chance to live.

BODY.P1: In the middle of this chaos, a stubborn, cheerful and unstoppable woman, Dr., a pioneer who dreamed of becoming a surgeon but turned to anesthesiology due to the sexist obstacles of the time. Virginia Apgar was about to step up. He would see not only the mother but also the baby, whom the medical world had considered almost "invisible" until that moment, as his patient, and he would turn the first minute of life into a branch of science.
BODY.P2: Dr. When Virginia Apgar graduated from Columbia University School of Medicine, her dream was to become a surgeon. Although she stood out with her intelligence and talent, one of the leading surgeons of the time told her the bitter truth: It was almost impossible for a woman to succeed in surgery, because male patients did not trust her and male colleagues would not accept her. He was devastated but didn't give up. Since he could not leave the operating room, he would then assume one of the most critical roles there: anesthesiology.
BODY.P3: And each time, he encountered the same tragic sight: While all the attention of the birth team was on the mother, breathless, blue, unresponsive babies were left aside to their fate. They needed a voice, an advocate. That voice, Dr. It would be Virginia Apgar.
BODY.P4: The Apgar Score forced the birth team to focus on the baby. Many infant deaths, which until then were called "divine" deaths, were actually caused by preventable respiratory distress. Dr. Virginia Apgar brought countless babies back to life with this simple test that can be performed in just 60 seconds.
BODY.P5: Dr. Virginia Apgar dedicated her life to the medical world's most vulnerable patients. He was a pioneer who broke down the barriers for women, an academician who turned anesthesiology into a branch of science, and most importantly, a hero who gave voice to the first breath of life.
BODY.P6: No matter where a baby is born today, his first step in life is taken by Dr. Virginia Apgar's legacy is judged by the simple but life-saving score she developed. He is the silent architect of the first "passing grade" given to each of us.

WALDO_NELSON.NAME: Dr. Waldo E. Nelson
WALDO_NELSON.TITLE: Master of Modern Pediatric Education

CONTENT.TEXT_B: After graduating from Johns Hopkins in 1925, Nelson decided to specialize in pediatrics and realized that pediatric medicine was still a developing field at that time. His approach, which emphasized that children are not just "little adults" but have their own physiological and psychological characteristics, was revolutionary in the field of pediatrics.
CONTENT.TEXT_C: After completing his residency in pediatrics in 1927, he began his academic career as a lecturer at New York University. He observed that during this period, information on pediatric diseases was not systematically organized and medical students needed comprehensive resources in this field.
CONTENT.TEXT_E: His work "Pediatrics: Principles and Practice", published in 1937, marked the beginning of a new era in pediatric education. This book was one of the first comprehensive resources that addressed pediatric diseases with a systematic approach and focused on practical applications.
CONTENT.TEXT_F: Nelson's vision became clearer when he became chairman of the department of pediatrics at Temple University in 1941. He argued that pediatrics was not only a field that treated diseases, but also a specialty that supported the healthy growth and development of children.
CONTENT.TEXT_G: "A good doctor treats anxious parents as well as the sick child. Because child health cannot be considered separately from family health. We must take a holistic approach."
CONTENT.TEXT_H: Nelson Textbook of Pediatrics: Birth of a Legend
CONTENT.TEXT_I: The year 1955 was the most important turning point in Nelson's career. The first edition of the "Nelson Textbook of Pediatrics" published this year made a huge impact on the world of pediatrics. This work was the first book to address childhood diseases with a systematic, comprehensive and scientific approach.
CONTENT.TEXT_J: The book not only focused on the diagnosis and treatment of diseases, but also covered in detail the normal growth and development processes of children, their nutritional needs, psychological development and communication methods with families.
CONTENT.TEXT_K: Nelson's approach considered child health from a holistic perspective. He emphasized the importance of children's emotional and social development as well as their physical health, and argued that pediatricians should support healthy development, not just diseases.
CONTENT.TEXT_M: Elected president of the American Academy of Pediatrics in 1963, Nelson played important roles in setting child health standards. During this period, he carried out pioneering work on the standardization of pediatric training curricula, improving the quality of specialty education and supporting research activities.
CONTENT.TEXT_N: Under Nelson's leadership, many innovations in the field of pediatrics were implemented. Establishing child health centers, disseminating the family-centered care approach and determining the training standards of pediatricians are among the important achievements of this period.
CONTENT.TEXT_O: Lasting Legacy and Impact on Today
CONTENT.TEXT_P: The Nelson Textbook of Pediatrics has been updated 21 times since its first publication, and each edition has attracted great attention in the world of pediatrics. The book has been translated into more than 50 languages ​​worldwide and used by millions of medical students and pediatricians.
CONTENT.TEXT_Q: It is possible to see traces of the principles determined by Nelson in children's hospitals operating around the world today. Family-centered care, treatment approaches that take into account the developmental characteristics of children, and comprehensive health assessments are concrete reflections of his vision.

WALDO_NELSON.QUOTE: Children are not small adults. They have medical needs of their own.

BODY.P1: Dr. is the author of the "Nelson Textbook of Pediatrics", which is the bedside book of millions of doctors around the world. Waldo E. Nelson is an American pediatrician who laid the foundations of modern pediatric education and developed contemporary approaches to child health. His works are still considered an integral part of pediatric education today.
BODY.P2: Born in New York City in 1898, Dr. Waldo E. Nelson was noted for his interest in medicine and education from his early years. Following the strong academic foundation he received at Yale University, the education he received at Johns Hopkins School of Medicine laid the foundation for his future success.
BODY.P3: When he passed away in 1997 at the age of 98, Dr. Waldo E. Nelson left behind not only books, but also a philosophy that changed the way children's health was approached. His “children are not little adults” approach remains a cornerstone of pediatric practice today.
BODY.P4: Dr. Waldo E. Nelson is remembered not only as a book author but also as the architect of modern pediatrics. He has shown that child health is not limited to just treating diseases, but supporting healthy growth and development is just as important.

SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.INDIVIDUAL_SUPPORT_DESC: In addition to group sessions, each parent receives 15-30 minutes of phone support once a week.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.INSPIRE_INFO: Part of INSPIRE strategy for ending violence against children
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.INTRO: Strengthen your relationship with your child, reduce behavioral problems, and increase family harmony.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.INTRO_LEAD_TEXT: Triple P has reached millions of families in over 30 countries worldwide, supported by 40 years of research and proven effectiveness as a parenting support system.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.LEVEL_GROUP_DESC: Small group training sessions for parents
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PHILOSOPHY_TEXT: One of Triple P's most innovative features is its multi-layered and flexible support system that responds to each family's unique needs. The program is built on the principle of "minimum sufficiency": providing families with as much support as they need to achieve their goals, but not providing more than necessary that would make them dependent on external help.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.POPULATION_IMPACT_DESC: Large-scale implementation studies show that Triple P can create positive changes at the community level.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PRINCIPLES_SUBTITLE: Five universal positive parenting principles taught at all levels of Triple P and forming the foundation of the program
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PRINCIPLE_1_DESC: Creating an encouraging environment that goes beyond protecting children from physical dangers, supporting their natural curiosity and allowing them to explore and learn.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PRINCIPLE_2_DESC: Parents are their children's first and most important teachers. Spending quality time, using descriptive praise, and giving positive responses to the child's interactions.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PRINCIPLE_3_DESC: Viewing discipline as a teaching opportunity, not punishment. Ensuring children take responsibility for their own behavior and learn to control themselves.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PRINCIPLE_4_DESC: Accepting that every child is unique and develops at their own pace. Eliminating the pressure created by perfectionist expectations.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PRINCIPLE_5_DESC: Parenting is a marathon. Taking care of your own physical, mental and emotional needs is a fundamental requirement of effective parenting.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PROGRAM_DESC: Throughout the program, parents watch video examples, participate in group discussions, and learn practical strategies they can apply at home. Weekly homework assignments ensure that learned techniques are transferred to daily life.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.PROGRAM_DURATION_DESC: One session per week for 8 weeks
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.REGISTRATION_DESC: Registration is open for our next group program
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SELF_EFFICACY_DESC: Encourages parents to trust their own problem-solving skills
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SELF_MANAGEMENT_DESC: Enables them to set their own goals and develop strategies
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SELF_REGULATION_DESC: Provides the ability to solve problems independently
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SESSION_1_CONTENT: Core principles of positive parenting, importance of child development, and strategies for creating a safe learning environment
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SESSION_2_CONTENT: Reinforcing desired behaviors, descriptive praise, and skill teaching through modeling
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SESSION_3_CONTENT: Effective discipline strategies, consistent consequences, and techniques for staying calm
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.SESSION_4_CONTENT: Proactive planning for high-risk situations, problem-solving skills, and continuing the parenting journey independently
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.STEPPING_STONES_DESC: For parents of children with disabilities
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.STEP_1_DESC: Creating a safe and engaging environment
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.STUDIES_DESC: Proven effectiveness through randomized controlled trials
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.TITLE: Triple P® Program
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.TOTAL_PUBLICATIONS_DESC: Scientific research and studies including peer-reviewed articles, books, and chapters
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.UNIVERSITY_DESC: Where it was developed and approved
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.UNIVERSITY_DESCRIPTION: The program developed at the University of Queensland by Prof. Matt Sanders and his team is based on scientific findings in social learning theory, cognitive behavioral therapy, and developmental psychology.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.WHAT_IS_DESC: Triple P – Positive Parenting Program® is a parenting and family support system designed to prevent and also treat behavioral and emotional problems in children and youth.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.WHAT_IS_DESC2: Developed by the University of Queensland, the program is based on over 35 years of clinical research. It is currently applied in more than 25 countries and has reached over 4 million children and families worldwide.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.WHAT_IS_DESC3: Triple P gives parents the skills they need to build stronger, healthier relationships with their children and feel confident in supporting their children's development.
SERVICE_TRIPLE_P.WHO_APPROVED_DESC: Program recommended by the World Health Organization

TESTIMONIALS.LABEL: Testimonials
TESTIMONIALS.SUBTITLE: We are happy to have served dozens of families with our 15 years of experience
TESTIMONIALS.TESTIMONIAL_1: Dr. Özlem is truly a special doctor who knows how to approach children. I feel much safer thanks to the care and patience she shows at every check-up of my son, and the detailed information she provides. I researched a lot when choosing a pediatrician, I definitely made the right choice.
TESTIMONIALS.TESTIMONIAL_2: The cleanliness and modern equipment of the clinic caught my attention. Thanks to Dr. Özlem's social pediatrics approach, my daughter's fear of doctors has completely disappeared. It's also very easy to make an appointment, and the assistants are very attentive and helpful.
TESTIMONIALS.TESTIMONIAL_3: My child had recurring infections. Now he is much healthier thanks to Dr. Özlem's accurate diagnosis and patient treatment. Both her knowledge and approach to children are perfect. We are very satisfied as a family.
TESTIMONIALS.TESTIMONIAL_4: We go for my son's growth and development follow-up. Dr. Özlem performs a very detailed examination every time and patiently answers every question we have. The clinic's atmosphere is also very comfortable and peaceful for children.
TESTIMONIALS.TESTIMONIAL_5: When we applied for my twin babies, Dr. Özlem supported us a lot. She solved our feeding problems and helped us establish sleep routines. She is truly an experienced and child-loving doctor. I recommend her to everyone.
TESTIMONIALS.TESTIMONIAL_6: We regularly go for my child's vaccination tracking. Dr. Özlem provides very detailed information about vaccines and never rushes. Our waiting time at the clinic is never long. The appointment system works very orderly.
TESTIMONIALS.TITLE: Reviews from Our Families
